# BP-5 negative: assigning Close error to a named variable preserves it
lang: go
file: BP-5-blank-identifier-safe.go
variant: stdlib
---
package sample

import "fmt"

type closer struct{}

func (closer) Close() error { return nil }

// CloseAndInspect preserves a Close error for its caller.
func CloseAndInspect(c closer) error {
	if err := c.Close(); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("close: %w", err)
	}
	return nil
}
